page.title: The Backbone of Quinfall's Economy
page.description: Professions are the second half of Quinfall. Between the player swinging a pickaxe and the one baking bread there's only a workbench — but the right chain of professions is what climbs you up the wealth ladder.

hero.title: Quinfall's Production Heart
hero.subtitle: 5 gathering trades, 5 processing benches, 7 crafting professions — 836 verified recipes total. Which bench builds what, when each profession unlocks T2/T3, and which tool tier opens which node — all here.

tools.title: 5 Tool Families · 3 Quality Tiers
tools.subtitle: Every gathering profession has its own tool. Higher tier tools harvest higher-grade nodes and improve rare-drop chance.

axe.name: Axe
axe.body: Felling logs from trees. Sturdy axe reaches mid-tier hardwoods, advanced axe unlocks rare species (elm, ebony).

pickaxe.name: Pickaxe
pickaxe.body: Strikes ore veins. Copper/iron with basic, silver/gold with sturdy, mithril veins need the advanced pickaxe.

sickle.name: Sickle
sickle.body: Harvests hemp, herbs and wild plants. Top-tier sickle reaches rare alchemy reagents and magic herbs.

fishingRod.name: Fishing Rod
fishingRod.body: River, lake and sea catches plus rare relics. Sturdy rod hits sea fish, advanced rod opens underwater relics.

huntingKnife.name: Hunting Knife
huntingKnife.body: Skins game — fur, raw hide, meat and bone. Advanced knife pushes rare-fur drop chance much higher.

flow.title: From Raw to Finished — 7 Chains
flow.subtitle: Three links each: Gather → Process → Craft. Run the whole chain solo or buy/sell links from other players.

metal.name: Metal Chain
metal.body: Mine the ore, smelt it into ingots, forge weapons or armour at the smithy.

wood.name: Wood Chain
wood.body: Fell logs in the forest, mill them into planks at the sawmill, make furniture or hafts at the carpenter.

leather.name: Leather Chain
leather.body: Skin a hunt, tan the hide into leather, cut light armour or harnesses at the tailor.

fabric.name: Fabric Chain
fabric.body: Pick wild hemp fibre, spin it into thread on a wheel, sew robes or mage outfits at the tailor.

food.name: Food Chain
food.body: Catch fish or prep game, take it to the cook's bench, bake group buff feasts.

gem.name: Gem Chain
gem.body: Mine rough gems, polish them at the cutting bench, set them into rings/earrings/necklaces at the jeweller.

essence.name: Essence Chain
essence.body: Gather raw essence in the wild, distill into element essence at the refiner, brew potions or spells at the alchemist.

milestones.title: What Unlocks at Which Level
milestones.subtitle: Threshold levels you should mark across all professions — miss one and your production chain stalls.

t1Open.body: Every profession reaches its T1 bench the moment your character spawns. Spam cheap recipes for fast XP.

t2Combat.body: Smithing / Tailoring T2 unlocks — stronger weapons and armour sets.

t2Alchemy.body: Alchemy T2: buff potions and elemental potions hit the market.

t2Smelt.body: Smelting + Carpentry T2 furnace opens — work silver/gold and build heavy furniture.

t3Smith.body: Smithing / Tailoring T3 — endgame recipes, the season meta gear is forged here.

t2Gem.body: Jewelry / Gem Cutting T2 — socketed jewelry recipes; the real market starts here.

t3Cook.body: Cooking / Carpentry / Smelting T3 — group raid feasts and mithril gear.

sturdyTool.body: Lvl 50 advanced tool tier — top rare-drop chance from any node.

t3Cap.body: Cooking and Jewelry T3 final recipes unlock.

t3Jewel.body: Jewelry top tier — endgame talismans and set jewelry are crafted.

workbench.title: T1, T2, T3 — The Three Workbench Grades
workbench.subtitle: As you level a profession, higher-tier workbenches unlock with new recipes, better outputs and longer craft times.

t1.label: STARTER
t1.headline: Basic Bench — Open at Lvl 1
t1.body: Available the moment your character spawns. Use the city's shared bench for free or place your own on personal land.
bullets: Works with copper, iron, hemp, raw meat — every starter material
Short craft times (3–24 s) and low XP — ideal for fast leveling
Every profession starts at T1; the prerequisite for higher tiers

t2.label: MID-TIER
t2.headline: Improved Bench — Profession Lvl 9–20
t2.body: Unlocks between profession levels 9 and 20 depending on the trade. Better gear, stronger potions, higher market prices.
bullets: Smithing / Tailoring at 9, Carpentry / Smelting at 20, Alchemy at 15
Craft time 2–3× longer, but XP scales with it
Building a T2 bench needs T1 outputs + the profession-level gate

t3.label: MASTER
t3.headline: Master Bench — Endgame Production
t3.body: Where the season meta is built. Recipes drop from rare books, materials travel by caravan, prices top the market.
bullets: 80–180 s per craft, but max stats and seasonal meta gear
Master benches need a large estate — guild membership recommended

matrix.title: 15 Professions, T1·T2·T3 Thresholds
matrix.subtitle: Recipe count, level range and tier-unlock level per profession — computed straight from items.json

fishing.name: Fishing
fishing.body: Rod + river/sea coordinates + patience. Cooking's raw feeder; rare relics are the market goldmine.

mining.name: Mining
mining.body: Pulls ore from deposits — copper, iron, silver, gold, mithril. The starting link for smelting and jewelry chains.

harvesting.name: Harvesting
harvesting.body: Hemp fibre, herbs and alchemy reagents. Shared feeder for spinning, alchemy and cooking.

hunting.name: Hunting
hunting.body: Skins game for hide, fur, meat and bone. Raw input for tanning and a meat source for cooking.

logging.name: Logging
logging.body: Turns gathered logs into planks, hafts, grips and arrow shafts. Foundation feed for blacksmithing and carpentry.

smelting.name: Smelting
smelting.body: Refines raw ore into usable ingots at high heat. Core supply for blacksmithing and jewelry.

tanning.name: Tanning
tanning.body: Converts a hunter's raw hide into usable leather. Essential for tailoring and carpentry.

spinning.name: Spinning
spinning.body: Spins wild-grown hemp fibre into thread. The raw material for tailoring; uses the spinning wheel.

gemcutting.name: Gem Cutting
gemcutting.body: Cuts and polishes raw gems from mining. Provides socket material for jewelry and engineering.

essence-refining.name: Essence Refining
essence-refining.body: Distills raw essences from gathering. The quiet partner of alchemy and engineering.

cooking.name: Cooking
cooking.body: Turns meat, fish, vegetables and spices into group feasts. Temporary stat buffs are critical in PvE/PvP.

alchemy.name: Alchemy
alchemy.body: Plant + essence + rare components → potions, elixirs and magic reagents. Demand peaks on PvP days.

blacksmithing.name: Blacksmithing
blacksmithing.body: Ingot + haft makes a weapon. The studio's most visible profession; the season meta blacksmith dominates the market.

tailoring.name: Tailoring
tailoring.body: Makes mage and ranger armor. Fed by spinning and tanning; high sale margins.

carpentry.name: Carpentry
carpentry.body: Home furniture, storage chests, workbench frames. The backbone of the housing system.

engineering.name: Engineering
engineering.body: Builds siege gear for guild war. Survives off trap sales between siege seasons.

jewelcrafting.name: Jewelry
jewelcrafting.body: Small slots, big effects. Pairs with gem cutting; top tier opens at lvl 60–70.

alchemy.name: Alchemy
alchemy.summary: Combine herbs, essences and rare reagents into potions, elixirs and arcane tinctures. Healing and buff potions are mandatory in PvE; in PvP, you're the one running the market.
alchemy.outputs: Health potions, mana potions, temporary stat-buff elixirs, revive draughts, antitoxins.
alchemy.leveling: At low level batch-craft cheap heals — they pay your tuition. At mid-tier shift to buff elixirs for margin. For top recipes walk hand-in-hand with essence-refining.
alchemy.moneyTip: On heavy PvP days buff demand spikes. Measure your hourly output and ride the demand curve.

cooking.name: Cooking
cooking.tagline: Players who eat buffs are happy players
cooking.summary: Meat, fish, vegetables, spice — turn it all into a banquet. Food gives temporary stat boosts; you're the most-wanted bench at the raid door.
cooking.outputs: Single-stat meals, multi-buff banquets, fast-bite snacks, caravan rations.
cooking.leveling: Spam single-stat meals like meat-and-bread early. At 30+ shift to banquets that feed half a party at once. Pair with a farmer to halve your gathering load.
cooking.moneyTip: Demand explodes the day a boss raid is announced. Schedule production around the raid calendar.

smelting.name: Smelting
smelting.tagline: Ore into ingots, dreams into weapons
smelting.summary: Take raw ore from the mine and pour it into ingots at heat. The base feed for blacksmithing and jewelcrafting; thin margin solo, but you sit at the start of the chain.
smelting.outputs: Iron, steel, silver, gold and mithril ingots.
smelting.leveling: Level mining first — supplying your own ore doubles your real margin. Smelt one ore type at a time in big batches for steady XP flow.
smelting.moneyTip: Per-ingot profit is small but throughput is high. Sell in bulk; this is volume work, not boutique.

woodcrafting.name: Woodcrafting
woodcrafting.summary: Refine raw logs into planks, hafts, grips and arrow shafts. Non-negotiable feed for blacksmithing and carpentry.
woodcrafting.outputs: Planks, weapon hafts, arrow shafts, furniture frames.
woodcrafting.leveling: Crafting hafts is the cheapest XP early. At top tier head deep into the woods for rare species — elm, ebony — and run a caravan back.
woodcrafting.moneyTip: Sell direct to a smith in bulk. Market prices wobble; a smith partnership locks in steady income.

engineering.name: Engineering
engineering.tagline: Machines for the siege, traps for the war
engineering.summary: Design mechanisms, traps, siege engines and caravan defense. Not a solo craft — leans on smelting, woodcrafting and gemcutting. But in a castle siege, the catapult crew gets paid.
engineering.outputs: Traps, ballistas, siege engines, automated rigs, gate-breakers.
engineering.leveling: Start with simple traps. As you approach top tier, guild and seasonal sieges put catapult demand at the very top of the market.
engineering.moneyTip: One catapult during a siege season can pay for a year. In off-season, run trap orders to keep the lights on.

blacksmithing.name: Blacksmithing
blacksmithing.tagline: The bench every weapon hand passes
blacksmithing.summary: Ingots and hafts become weapons. The most visible profession in the game — the sword on someone's back is your signature. A top-tier smith makes the season's meta weapon.
blacksmithing.outputs: One-handed and two-handed weapons, heavy armor sets, shields, war hammers.
blacksmithing.leveling: Grind cheap starter weapons for XP, then move to commissioned orders at 30+. Owning the mining + smelting chain end-to-end multiplies your margin.
blacksmithing.moneyTip: Patches shift the meta. The smith who stocks the new meta weapon at season open eats orders for days.

jewelcrafting.name: Jewelcrafting
jewelcrafting.summary: Rings, necklaces, earrings, bracelets — small slots, large effects. Walks side by side with gemcutting; every piece comes with one or two sockets.
jewelcrafting.leveling: Single-stat trinkets early. Double-socket recipes open at 50+ — the real market starts there.
jewelcrafting.moneyTip: Partner with a gemcutter; together you reach the top margin tier.

gemcutting.name: Gemcutting
gemcutting.summary: Cut and polish raw gems from the mine into socket-ready stones. The quiet partner of jewelcrafting and engineering.
gemcutting.outputs: Socket-ready ruby, sapphire, emerald, opal, diamond — each tuned to a different stat.
gemcutting.leveling: Batch one gem type, keep the bench warm. At top tier, special cuts (oval, marquise) push extra stats.
gemcutting.moneyTip: Top-tier cuts run the meta in PvP builds. Right gem + right cut is the final stamp on every endgame piece.

essence-refining.name: Essence Refining
essence-refining.summary: Distill raw essences from gathering professions into the bedrock feed for alchemy and engineering. Quiet, but a critical link in the chain.
essence-refining.outputs: Magic dust, mana essence, elemental essences, upgrade reagents.
essence-refining.leveling: Spam basic essence recipes early. At mid-tier shift to elemental essences (fire, frost, storm) — that's where alchemists start lining up.
essence-refining.moneyTip: Elemental essence depends on recipe books. Whoever guards recipe access can monopolize the market.

leatherworking.name: Leatherworking
leatherworking.summary: Take hunter's hides, tan and stitch them into light armor, bags, sheaths and belts. Your signature is on every survivor's back.
leatherworking.outputs: Light armor sets, bags, weapon sheaths, belts, gloves.
leatherworking.leveling: Bag size is what every player buys in their first 50 hours — spam starter bags, XP and income flow side by side.
leatherworking.moneyTip: Two or three bags per new player is guaranteed. On a fresh server, leatherworking is the fastest profession to break even.

thread-weaving.name: Thread Weaving
thread-weaving.tagline: The first stop on the way to cloth
thread-weaving.summary: Spin fibers from harvesters into thread. Non-negotiable feed for tailoring; you don't get rich on direct sales — you get rich in partnership with a tailor.
thread-weaving.outputs: Cotton thread, linen thread, silk, arcane-laced thread.
thread-weaving.leveling: Batch one fiber at a time. Arcane-laced thread unlocks at 50+ and prices multiply there.
thread-weaving.moneyTip: Sign a long-term swap deal with a tailor — locks in your day's output and shields you from market noise.

tailoring.name: Tailoring
tailoring.summary: Thread to cloth, cloth to robes, cloaks and caster armor. The non-negotiable supplier for staff and life-staff players.
tailoring.outputs: Robes, cloaks, cloth armor, caster hats, mage hoods.
tailoring.leveling: Spam basic robes at low tier. At 40+ elemental-resist cloth opens — mandatory for any raid healer.
tailoring.moneyTip: Lifestaff numbers spike in raid season. Tailoring carries gravity in those weeks.

carpentry.name: Carpentry
carpentry.tagline: A house with character is worth more
carpentry.summary: Furniture, structural pieces, decorative builds. Player housing demand is steady; a good carpenter is the soft, reliable winner of the market.
carpentry.outputs: Chairs, tables, cupboards, shelves, decor pieces, guild-hall components.
carpentry.leveling: Start with chair-and-table sets. At 30+ rare wood species unlock signature recipes.
carpentry.moneyTip: Guild halls and player housing are the steady demand. Carpenters always have buyers.

logging.name: Logging
logging.tagline: The axe-fall sound at the heart of the economy
logging.summary: Walk into the forest, fell the tree, drag the log. Low startup, high demand — the textbook gathering loop. Start of the wood chain.
logging.leveling: Don't park in one zone — logs are respawn-bound. Rotate between forests for steady throughput.
logging.moneyTip: Level a logger pet — up to +50% rate, real impact on hourly income.

mining.name: Mining
mining.tagline: The start of every ore river
mining.summary: Swing the pickaxe, pull ore from rock. The raw input for smelting, gemcutting and essence refining. Low startup, durable income.
mining.outputs: Copper, iron, silver, gold, mithril, raw gemstones.
mining.leveling: Run heavy nodes in respawn rotation. Map markers reveal cycle patterns — exploit them.
mining.moneyTip: Run your own smelter — every ingot's margin stays with you. Otherwise lock in a smith partnership.

harvesting.name: Harvesting
harvesting.tagline: Herbs, flowers, crops — the green is yours
harvesting.summary: Gather wild plants, mushrooms, herbs and crops. Base feed for alchemy, cooking and thread-weaving.
harvesting.outputs: Healing herbs, mushrooms, flowers, crops, mulberry leaf.
harvesting.leveling: Level a harvest pet — double-drop chance flips your hourly. Pairs with farming naturally.
harvesting.moneyTip: Supply an alchemist directly — over-market deals at +20% are common.

fishing.name: Fishing
fishing.summary: Freshwater, sea, sunken-ship spots — fishing scales over time. Slow pace, but a top-tier catch is a windfall.
fishing.outputs: Freshwater fish, sea fish, rare ocean species, treasure-chest fragments.
fishing.leveling: Buy the best rod first — bite speed is the bottleneck. Level a fishing pet on top.
fishing.moneyTip: Rare catch + sea trading is the high horizontal market — one ocean run can open the door to a year of income.

hunting.name: Hunting
hunting.summary: Hunt wild beasts for hide, meat and rare trophies. The single supplier for cook + leatherworker; a profitable bridge.
hunting.outputs: Deer hide, bear pelt, wolf coat, meat, rare trophies.
hunting.leveling: Match prey level to yours. Top-tier hunts are boss-class — bring a hunting partner.
hunting.moneyTip: Hunt season makes waves. Selling hide and meat as a bundle doubles your run profit.

farming.name: Farming
farming.summary: Buy land, plant seeds, harvest crops. Slow, but the artery of passive income. Direct feed for cooking and thread-weaving.
farming.outputs: Grain, vegetables, fruit, spice, mulberry leaf.
farming.leveling: Single-crop rotation in your first 20 levels. Then expand to multi-plot management; land ownership compounds.
farming.moneyTip: Sign a direct supply contract with a cook. Farming market noise is loud; lock in via contract.

breeding.name: Breeding
breeding.tagline: Strengthen the line, set the price ceiling
breeding.summary: Raise mounts and pets. Top-tier rare bloodlines are the highest-priced single items on the market. Long-term investment, top-tier return.
breeding.outputs: Mount lineages, pet lineages, advanced bloodlines, seasonal animals.
breeding.leveling: Balance breeding for the first 30 levels. Then invest in rare traits — one legendary mount can pay back a year of effort.
breeding.moneyTip: The seasonal meta mount usually peaks in launch month. Dedicate your first 2 weeks of production to it.

caravan-trading.name: Caravan Trade
caravan-trading.tagline: Move goods between cities — let the road price the risk
caravan-trading.summary: Move goods between cities. Bandit ambush, weather, blocked roads — risk every step. But on the right route, hourly income breaks the chart.
caravan-trading.outputs: Inter-city price gap is your profit. Top-tier crafted goods + rare spice = best margin.
caravan-trading.leveling: Try short routes first. Run with a guild on long routes — protection plus shared reward.
caravan-trading.moneyTip: Caravan events during seasons cap profit. The golden ticket for hybrid PvE/PvP players.

sea-trading.name: Sea Trade
sea-trading.summary: Run cargo between ports. Riskier than caravans, more profitable. Pirates and storms — escort or guild support is mandatory.
sea-trading.outputs: Inter-port price gaps, rare ocean goods, seasonal sea-quest rewards.
sea-trading.leveling: Small boat → mid-tonnage cargo → large merchantman. Each step is a big buy and a big payoff.
sea-trading.moneyTip: Memorize the sea map; track wind and storm charts. The captain who knows them owns the market.

treasure-hunting.name: Treasure Hunting
treasure-hunting.summary: Old maps, scattered clues, hidden chests. A hobby for casual players; a high-variance season for professionals.
treasure-hunting.outputs: Rare costumes, unique trinkets, old-school recipe books, treasure-cache parts.
treasure-hunting.leveling: Early maps come for free; later ones are traded. Loot is uneven — some weeks dry, some weeks loaded.
treasure-hunting.moneyTip: If a single map drops top-tier, that one week beats a mid-tier player's three.

chain.heading: PRODUCTION CHAIN
chain.title: Who Feeds Whom
chain.subtitle: Quinfall professions don't run alone. Gather → refine → assemble → sell. The most common flows below.

metal.name: The Iron Lane
metal.description: From mine to catapult — the classic metal chain.

wood.name: The Wood Lane
wood.description: Logs to furniture — hafts, chairs and siege towers all start here.

fabric.name: The Cloth Lane
fabric.description: Harvest → thread → cloth → robe. The healer's and the mage's footsteps walk this lane.

leather.name: The Leather Lane
leather.description: The lone path that turns the hunter's pelt into light armor and bags.

consumable.name: The Consumable Lane
consumable.description: Where the kitchen and alchemy meet — the buffed player's name is on every chart.

1.title: Run a paired profession
1.body: One gathering + one crafting doubles your margin because you're not buying raw materials. Mining + Smelting, Logging + Woodcrafting, Harvesting + Alchemy.

2.title: Match your pet to your profession
2.body: If you're gathering, your pet is an enhancer — +50% rate and +5% double drops compound your hourly income.

3.title: Read the season calendar
3.body: Guild war, siege season, raid announcement — each spikes a different profession's demand. Catch the wave; ride it.

4.title: Lock in bulk supply contracts
4.body: Beat one-off sales by signing a regular buyer. Steady supply beats piecemeal flipping every time.

5.title: Hunt recipe books
5.body: Most top-tier recipes live in hidden books. A single recipe can hand you a market monopoly.

6.title: Move your bench out of town
6.body: City benches are slow at peak times. The guild hall or your own land is where real production happens.
